sources.name: SEC EDGAR full-text search API (efts.sec.gov) — re-activation of stubbed 13D/13G leg
sources.what_it_provides: Full-text search over all EDGAR filings since 2001 with form-type and date filters (forms=SC+13D,SC+13G, 8-K, etc.), returning JSON hit lists with CIK, filer display_names, form, file_date, accession id. Enables activist beneficial-ownership (13D/13G) detection — the missing Leg 3 of the shipped 3-leg M&A pre-announcement aggregator — plus arbitrary event scans (going-private, merger language).
sources.free_tier: No authentication or API keys (official EDGAR API page). Fair-access policy verbatim: 'Current max request rate: 10 requests/second' + 'Please declare your user agent in request headers'. Public-domain US government data. LIVE-VERIFIED 2026-07-10: HTTP 200 with declared UA on form- and date-filtered queries (e.g. forms=SC+13D&dateRange=custom returned scored JSON hits); the repo stub's documented 403 reproduces only when the UA header is absent.
sources.roi_rationale: Highest P&L-per-effort of all candidates: backend/services/ma_preannounce_screen.py Legs 1 (options surge) and 2 (opportunistic insiders) are already live, and the aggregator pays +10% composite-score boost on 2-leg convergence — Leg 3 is a stub returning [] purely because of a missing User-Agent header. The repo already has the exact UA + 8 req/s discipline in backend/alt_data/f13.py to copy. Effort is a ~50-line function; Augustin et al. document informed-trading footprints ahead of M&A announcements, and 13D filings are the strongest public confirmation leg.
sources.integration_point: backend/services/ma_preannounce_screen.py::_fetch_13d_filings_for (line 55 stub, TODO phase-28.16-followup-13d-edgar) — reuse SEC_HEADERS from backend/tools/sec_insider.py; consumed by compute_ma_preannounce_signals in the autonomous_loop screener overlay.

sources.name: Finnhub free-tier endpoints not yet consumed (insider transactions/sentiment, recommendation trends, per-symbol news, earnings surprises, as-reported financials)
sources.url: https://finnhub.io/docs/api (per-endpoint premium flags embedded in official docs; rate limits: https://finnhub.io/docs/api/rate-limit)
sources.what_it_provides: Codebase uses exactly 2 of Finnhub's free endpoints (/news?category=general, /calendar/earnings). Verified free-flagged (premium=null) in the official docs metadata and unused: /stock/insider-transactions, /stock/insider-sentiment (MSPR aggregate), /stock/recommendation (analyst buy/hold/sell trend counts — international coverage), /company-news (per-symbol, 1yr history), /stock/metric (basic financials), /stock/earnings (EPS surprise history), /stock/financials-reported, /stock/filings. Premium-gated (do NOT plan on): price-target, upgrade-downgrade, ownership, social-sentiment, congressional-trading.
sources.free_tier: Free API key at signup (already provisioned: settings.finnhub_api_key). Official docs Limits section verbatim: 'On top of all plan's limit, there is a 30 API calls/second limit' with 429 on exceed; free-plan per-minute quota is rendered client-side on the pricing page (commonly 60 calls/min) — repo already runs a leaky-bucket limiter via get_rate_limiter('finnhub'). Free/premium split verified per-endpoint from docs metadata 2026-07-10. Free tier is for personal use — fits this single-Mac paper-trading deployment.
sources.roi_rationale: Near-zero integration effort: key, rate limiter, retry-with-backoff, api_call_log telemetry, and the adapter registry pattern all exist. Analyst revision breadth currently rides solely on fragile unofficial yfinance upgrades_downgrades scraping (analyst_revisions.py) — recommendation-trends gives an official, keyed second source for the phase-28.1 Sharpe~1.6 overlay; insider-sentiment (MSPR) cross-checks the CMP opportunistic-insider screen; earnings surprises feed PEAD SUE without LLM cost.
sources.integration_point: backend/services/analyst_revisions.py (ensemble/replace yfinance upgrades_downgrades with /stock/recommendation), backend/services/insider_signal_screen.py (MSPR cross-check), backend/econ_calendar/sources/finnhub_earnings.py (sibling fetch for /stock/earnings surprises), backend/news/sources/finnhub.py (per-symbol /company-news param).

sources.name: Korea DART OpenAPI (Financial Supervisory Service)
sources.what_it_provides: Canonical KR regulatory data for all KOSPI/KOSDAQ listed corps: disclosure filings search (list.json), quarterly/annual financial statements (fnlttSinglAcntAll — full single-company accounts in KRW), executive/major-shareholder ownership reports (KR insider dealing), major-issue reports (M&A, capital changes), paid-in capital events. JSON/XML; English-language endpoint mirror exists.
sources.free_tier: Official terms Art. 11 verbatim: '금융감독원이 제공하는 서비스는 원칙적으로 무료입니다' (services are free in principle). Requires 40-char crtfc_key (instant online issuance). Usage cap published on site; official dev guide states error code 020 'generally occurs for requests exceeding 20,000건' — i.e. ~20,000 requests/day per key; max 100 corp_codes per single request. Verified on official opendart.fss.or.kr pages 2026-07-10.
sources.roi_rationale: The KR sleeve trades LIVE with ZERO Korean fundamentals/filings/insider coverage — everything rides on yfinance, and the away-week KRW/USD unit error ($1.63 quadrillion market cap rendered; 066570.KS BUY stopped out -9.7%) is a realized P&L loss from exactly this gap. DART gives native-KRW ground truth for the phase-60.3 data_integrity pre-check (market-cap verification pre-LLM) plus the KR analog of the US insider edge (82bps/month CMP effect) that currently exists only for US names. Effort is moderate (corp_code mapping file + one ingester mirroring f13.py), impact is an entire live market sleeve going from blind to covered.
sources.integration_point: New backend/alt_data/dart.py mirroring backend/alt_data/f13.py (persist to pyfinagent_data.alt_* BQ tables); consumed by backend/services/data_integrity.py (native KRW market-cap/currency verification for the phase-60.3 integrity gate) and a KR branch of backend/services/insider_signal_screen.py.

sources.name: SEC XBRL data APIs — companyfacts / companyconcept / frames (data.sec.gov)
sources.what_it_provides: Structured point-in-time US-GAAP/IFRS fundamentals for every 10-K/10-Q/8-K/20-F filer: every reported fact with period, unit, accession, and filed date (enabling true PIT joins); frames API gives one fact per entity per calendar quarter for cross-sectional factors; nightly companyfacts.zip bulk file covers the whole universe in one download. Codebase currently touches only /submissions and Archives — the XBRL APIs are entirely unused.
sources.free_tier: Official API page verbatim: 'These APIs do not require any authentication or API keys to access'; updated real-time ('xbrl APIs... typical processing delay of under a minute'); same fair-access policy: max 10 requests/second + declared User-Agent; public-domain. Bulk ZIP republished nightly ~3:00 a.m. ET. Verified on sec.gov 2026-07-10.
sources.roi_rationale: Directly implements the gap the project's own audit chose: backend/data_audit/gaps.json (alt_credit_and_spending) recommends 'build a 10-Q revenue-surprise signal from existing sec_edgar data'. XBRL actuals (Revenues, EPS) vs trailing history give a deterministic SUE/revenue-surprise factor with $0 LLM cost, complementing the LLM-scored PEAD press-release sentiment already live; filed-date stamps make it backtest-safe (no yfinance revision/survivorship leakage), and the nightly bulk ZIP fits the 10 req/s budget trivially. Strengthens an already-working PEAD edge rather than adding an unproven one.
sources.integration_point: backend/services/pead_signal.py (add XBRL SUE/revenue-surprise leg beside the Haiku sentiment-surprise; cache dir pattern already exists) and backend/tools/quant_model.py fundamentals features; CIK resolution reuses backend/tools/sec_insider.py::_resolve_cik.

sources.name: ECB Data Portal API (SDMX 2.1 REST)
sources.what_it_provides: All ECB statistical dataflows keyless: EXR (daily ECB reference FX incl. EUR/USD), YC (euro-area AAA yield curve — spot rates incl. 10Y/2Y for slope/inversion), EST (€STR), ICP (HICP inflation), MIR (bank interest rates), plus policy-rate series. Content negotiation supports application/json and text/csv, not just SDMX-ML.
sources.free_tier: Official help page: 'The ECB SDMX 2.1 RESTful web service offers programmatic access to the statistical data and metadata disseminated via the ECB Data Portal.' No API key or registration for data retrieval; no published request quota. LIVE-VERIFIED 2026-07-10: HTTP 200 keyless on data-api.ecb.europa.eu YC 10Y spot-rate series; 406 response enumerated accepted formats incl. application/json and text/csv.
sources.roi_rationale: The EU sleeve (DAX-40) trades live, but macro regime detection and the econ calendar are 100% US-sourced (FRED releases + Fed FOMC scrape + US CPI/NFP) — EU positions are gated by the WRONG central bank's regime. Euro-area yield-curve slope, €STR path, and HICP releases are the EU analogs of signals already proven useful in macro_regime.py; also adds an independent EUR/USD fallback to the yfinance+FRED FX chain that values EU book NAV. Moderate effort (one SDMX-CSV fetcher, registry pattern exists), meaningful risk-gating impact on a live sleeve.
sources.integration_point: backend/econ_calendar/sources/ new ecb_releases.py registered via backend/econ_calendar/registry.py (siblings: fred_releases.py, fed_scrape.py); EUR regime inputs into backend/services/macro_regime.py; third FX fallback in backend/services/fx_rates.py::_CCY_FRED pattern.

sources.name: Wikimedia Pageviews API (AQS REST)
sources.what_it_provides: Per-article daily/monthly pageview counts since July 2015 for any Wikipedia article (company pages as attention proxy), with agent=user filtering to exclude bots. Established retail-attention/search-interest literature analog to Google Trends, with stable JSON REST and no scraping fragility.
sources.free_tier: No API key. Official REST spec global rules verbatim: 'Limit your clients to no more than 200 requests/s to this API' and 'Set a unique User-Agent or Api-User-Agent header'. Content CC-BY-SA 3.0 (attribution — fine for internal quant use). LIVE-VERIFIED 2026-07-10: keyless per-article daily JSON returned for en.wikipedia/Nvidia.
sources.roi_rationale: The project's own gaps.json (search_interest) already picked this: 'add Wikipedia pageviews as backup (free; zero auth)' — because the live Google Trends path rides pytrends, whose upstream was archived 2025-04-17, and backend/alt_data/google_trends.py remains a scaffold with live fetch deferred. Trivial effort (no auth, generous limits, one URL template), modest standalone alpha but de-risks the entire attention-signal class and feeds the existing IC evaluation harness so it can be promoted/killed on measured IC, consistent with the north star's OOS-robustness gates.
sources.integration_point: backend/alt_data/ new wiki_pageviews.py sibling to google_trends.py scaffold (persist to pyfinagent_data.alt_* per the phase-7 table pattern), IC-evaluated by backend/alt_data/features.py over the {5,20,63}-day forward-return windows.

sources.name: FRED — missing regime/liquidity series on the existing key (INDPRO, NFCI, WALCL, WTREGEN, RRPONTSYD, DTWEXBGS, T10Y3M)
sources.what_it_provides: Series the repo does not fetch that map directly onto macro_regime: INDPRO (industrial production — already listed in _REGIME_SERIES at macro_regime.py:43 but NEVER fetched by fred_data.py::SERIES, so the regime LLM silently runs on 6 of its 7 intended series); NFCI (Chicago Fed weekly financial conditions); WALCL/WTREGEN/RRPONTSYD (H.4.1 Fed balance sheet, TGA, ON-RRP — the standard net-liquidity construction WALCL−WTREGEN−RRPONTSYD, published Thursdays ~16:30 ET); DTWEXBGS (broad dollar index — risk-off proxy directly relevant to live EU/KR FX exposure); T10Y3M (Estrella-Mishkin recession spread).
sources.free_tier: Free API key (already configured as FRED_API_KEY); 120 requests/minute rate limit; full data access with no paid tier; ToS prohibits replicating the FRED site experience, internal analytical use fine (fred.stlouisfed.org/docs/api/api_key.html, /docs/api/terms_of_use.html; H.4.1 release verified at fred.stlouisfed.org/release?rid=20 and /series/WALCL, /series/WTREGEN).
sources.roi_rationale: Highest ROI-per-effort of anything found: ~10 lines appended to two existing dicts plus prompt thresholds, no new dependency, no new auth, and it REPAIRS an existing latent defect (INDPRO expected-but-missing) in the module that scales every candidate score daily. Net liquidity and NFCI are the most-cited risk-on/off drivers absent from the current 7-series snapshot; dollar index adds the FX beat to regime detection.
sources.integration_point: backend/tools/fred_data.py::SERIES + backend/services/macro_regime.py::_REGIME_SERIES and _build_prompt thresholds. Live path only — regime uses a 24h file cache (_CACHE_PATH), zero BQ writes. Do NOT add these to backend/backtest/data_ingestion.py::FRED_SERIES, which is the sole writer of the FROZEN historical_macro table.

sources.name: CFTC Commitments of Traders — Public Reporting Environment (Socrata SODA API)
sources.what_it_provides: Weekly futures positioning (Fridays 15:30 ET, as-of Tuesday): Traders-in-Financial-Futures dataset gives dealer/asset-manager/leveraged-fund net positions in S&P 500, Nasdaq-100, Treasury, VIX and EUR FX futures; Legacy + Disaggregated variants cover commodities incl. crude. Positioning extremes (percentile of net non-commercial length) are a documented contrarian/crowding regime input.
sources.free_tier: Completely free via web and API; CFTC states it is 'not providing tokens to use the Public Reporting APIs' and unrestricted use is generally permitted (cftc.gov/MarketReports/CommitmentsofTraders). Socrata platform: without app token requests share an IP-throttled pool; a free Socrata app token removes throttling for non-abusive use (dev.socrata.com/docs/app-tokens). CSV/JSON, history to 1986/2006.
sources.roi_rationale: The positioning beat is ENTIRELY missing from the repo (grep: zero CFTC/COT references), and the only positioning-shaped module that exists — backend/alt_data/etf_flows.py — is a dead scaffold whose fetch_issuer_page returns {} (flagged as re-activation candidate; COT delivers institutional positioning for less effort than finishing the issuer-page scraper). Weekly cadence matches the daily-cycle trader well: one fetch per week, one new signal dimension orthogonal to everything currently ingested.
sources.integration_point: New backend/alt_data/cot.py cloning the congress.py/finra_short.py ensure_table+upsert pattern into a NEW table pyfinagent_data.alt_cftc_cot (historical_macro stays frozen); consumed as a macro_regime.py post-process tilt reusing the generic _apply_gpr_tilt helper (it only reads above_threshold) and IC-evaluated via backend/alt_data/features.py.

sources.name: ECB Data Portal API (SDMX 2.1 REST) — euro-area regime series + EUR reference rates
sources.what_it_provides: CISS composite indicator of systemic stress — the euro-area analogue of the VIX+HY-OAS pair, DAILY series updated Mondays (dataset CISS, e.g. CISS.D.U2..SS_CIN.IDX; US and GB variants also exist); €STR, deposit facility rate, HICP, euro-area yield curves; official daily EUR reference FX rates (a third fallback for fx_rates.py behind yfinance/FRED). Governing Council decisions feed an ECB calendar source.
sources.free_tier: Free, NO registration or API key for the SDMX 2.1 REST service (data-api.ecb.europa.eu); account only needed for personalization features; content negotiation returns SDMX-ML/SDMX-JSON/CSV; no published rate limit — portal intermittently returned 503 during verification, so implement polite retry/backoff and the fail-open pattern used by _fetch_gpr_acts (data.ecb.europa.eu/help/getting-data-web-services-sdmx).
sources.roi_rationale: EU paper trading has been live since 2026-06-01 (benchmark ^GDAXI) yet grep shows ZERO euro-area macro anywhere — screener.py:319 applies the US-only regime multiplier to EU candidates. CISS alone gives a single daily stress number that plugs into the existing threshold-prompt design; this is the largest coverage hole relative to live capital allocation.
sources.integration_point: New backend/tools/ecb_data.py mirroring fred_data.py's shape, feeding an EU block in backend/services/macro_regime.py::_build_prompt (file-cache, no BQ) — persistence, if wanted, in a NEW table (e.g. pyfinagent_data.macro_eu_series), never historical_macro; ECB meeting dates as a new source in backend/econ_calendar/sources/ via the register() pattern; EUR reference rate as third fallback in backend/services/fx_rates.py::_CCY_FRED chain.

sources.name: Bank of Korea ECOS Open API
sources.what_it_provides: Official Korean macro: BOK base rate, CPI, USD/KRW official rate, FX reserves, money supply, trade balance — the KR regime inputs, plus Monetary Policy Committee decision context. Korea's export-heavy, chip-cyclical market makes local rates/CPI/FX-reserve series genuinely distinct from US proxies.
sources.free_tier: Free with a registered API key (application at ecos.bok.or.kr/api/#/AuthKeyApply); JSON/XML responses. CAVEAT verified honestly: the numeric daily quota is documented only inside the Korean-language JS portal and could NOT be independently confirmed from a fetchable official English page — commonly wrapped by open-source clients (CRAN 'ecos', github.com/seokhoonj/ecos) without stated limits; verify the quota at key-issuance time before scheduling fetch cadence.
sources.roi_rationale: Same structural hole as ECB but for KR: trading live since 2026-06-01 (benchmark ^KS11), zero Korean macro in the repo, US regime multiplier applied to KR candidates. Slightly lower rank than ECB because the KR book is smaller and the portal/key friction is higher (Korean-language docs), but it is the only free primary source for BOK-quality Korean series (FRED's IMF/OECD-sourced KR mirrors were widely discontinued in 2024).
sources.integration_point: New backend/tools/bok_ecos.py feeding a KR block in backend/services/macro_regime.py (file-cache pattern); optional persistence to a NEW table pyfinagent_data.macro_kr_series (historical_macro frozen); BOK MPC meeting dates as a new backend/econ_calendar/sources/ registry source.

sources.name: US Treasury FiscalData API (Daily Treasury Statement + Treasury auction results)
sources.what_it_provides: Daily Treasury Statement: DAILY operating cash balance (TGA) — sharpens the weekly FRED WTREGEN average into a daily net-liquidity signal; Treasury Securities Auctions data: bid-to-cover ratios, high yield, upcoming auction schedule — auction 'tails' are a rates-stress event marker, and the auction calendar itself is a known vol-event for equities.
sources.free_tier: Fully open REST API: no user account, registration, token or API key required for GET requests; base URL https://api.fiscaldata.treasury.gov/services/api/fiscal_service/; JSON/CSV/XML; no documented rate limit (fiscaldata.treasury.gov/api-documentation/ and dataset pages for daily-treasury-statement; US government work, public domain).
sources.roi_rationale: Zero references in repo. Cheapest possible auth story (none) and it compounds source #1: daily TGA + FRED WALCL/RRP turns net liquidity from weekly to daily resolution. Auction dates/results slot into the already-built econ_calendar registry exactly like fred_releases.py did. Ranked below ECB/BOK because it deepens an existing US signal rather than opening uncovered live-market exposure.
sources.integration_point: Daily TGA into the macro_regime.py net-liquidity computation alongside the new FRED series (file-cache, no BQ; historical_macro frozen); auction schedule/results as a new source class in backend/econ_calendar/sources/ (registry.register pattern, event_type='treasury_auction'), optionally persisted to a NEW pyfinagent_data table.

sources.name: EIA Open Data API v2 (Weekly Petroleum Status — crude/product inventories)
sources.what_it_provides: Weekly US crude oil + gasoline/distillate inventories (Weekly Petroleum Status Report, Wednesdays 10:30 ET) and natural gas storage — the supply-side complement to the price-side WTI momentum z-score already live in macro_regime (phase-28.6 CL=F trigger). Inventory-surprise direction vs 5-year average is an orthogonal energy-regime input the price series cannot see.
sources.free_tier: Free API key via registration (api.eia.gov v2); 'EIA data is provided free of charge'; JSON; bulk downloads updated twice daily; usage governed by the API Terms of Service and EIA copyright/reuse policy (US government data, reusable); rate limits not stated on the open-data page — hourly per-key throttling exists in practice, trivially cleared at one weekly fetch (eia.gov/opendata/).
sources.roi_rationale: Lowest rank of the six: it upgrades one already-working sector tilt rather than filling a coverage hole, but the marginal effort is the smallest kind the codebase supports — the generic _apply_gpr_tilt helper only reads above_threshold from a trigger dict, so an inventory-z-score trigger is a near-copy of _fetch_crude_momentum with a file cache and one settings flag, default-OFF per house pattern.
sources.integration_point: backend/services/macro_regime.py — new _fetch_eia_inventories() helper next to _fetch_crude_momentum(), feeding the existing _apply_gpr_tilt(parsed, info, settings.crude_momentum_sector_etfs) XLE-tilt path; file-cache under backend/services/_cache/, no BQ writes (historical_macro untouched).

sources.name: FINRA Consolidated Short Interest via Query API (replaces dead legacy CSV)
sources.what_it_provides: Bi-monthly equity short interest per symbol: currentShortPositionQuantity, previousShortPositionQuantity, averageDailyVolumeQuantity, daysToCoverQuantity, settlementDate, marketClassCode (verified live: GET api.finra.org/data/group/otcMarket/name/consolidatedShortInterest returned data rows unauthenticated). Also hosts regShoDaily (200 unauth) mirroring the daily short-volume CDN files. ANSWER TO THE TASK QUESTION: we have BOTH FINRA feeds -- daily short volume (backend/alt_data/finra_short.py, real, CDN file verified 200 today, but its BQ table alt_finra_short_volume has zero consumers) AND bi-monthly short interest (backend/services/short_interest.py, which IS live-consumed by the screener) -- but the bi-monthly source URL cdn.finra.org/equity/regsho/monthly/shrt{date}.csv now returns 403 (verified with the module's exact User-Agent, for 2026 AND 2025 settlement dates) and the local cache dir backend/tools/_cache/short_interest/ is empty, so the live exclusion filter is silently degraded to a 50-ticker yfinance fallback.
sources.free_tier: Public datasets queryable WITHOUT credentials (verified HTTP 200 with data, limit param honored). Free individual account registration grants 'Public' credential type. Rate limits per developer.finra.org/docs: 1,200 synchronous requests/min per IP, 5,000 records max per sync request, 20 async req/min per dataset per account. No fee for public equity data; firm/organization credential tiers are the paid ones.
sources.roi_rationale: Highest certainty-of-impact per line of code in this list: the consumer already exists and is live (screener exclusion at short_interest_threshold=0.10, grounded in Boehmer-Jones-Zhang 2008: top-decile shorted stocks underperform 1.16%/month), and it is provably running blind today. A URL+parser swap (JSON instead of pipe-CSV; compute float-short from currentShortPositionQuantity / yfinance float, or switch the threshold to daysToCoverQuantity which arrives ready-made) restores a working risk filter across the full US universe. This is a repair of a silent SecretStr-class degradation, not a speculative new signal -- zero regression risk to the working engine.
sources.integration_point: backend/services/short_interest.py::_try_download_finra_csv (swap to Query API endpoint; keep the 14-day cache + fail-open contract) -> consumed unchanged by backend/tools/screener.py exclusion filter; optionally also backfills the orphaned pyfinagent_data.alt_finra_short_volume table via backend/alt_data/finra_short.py

sources.name: CBOE delayed index quotes + daily market statistics (VIX term structure and put/call ratios)
sources.what_it_provides: Market-wide daily put/call ratios (total, index, equity-only, ETP, VIX) from the free daily market statistics page, plus the full VIX term structure via keyless delayed-quote JSON: cdn.cboe.com/api/global/delayed_quotes/quotes/_VIX9D.json and _VIX3M.json (verified live today: VIX9D=11.73, VIX3M=18.74, 15-min delayed, no auth) and full daily history CSVs for backtest -- cdn.cboe.com/api/global/us_indices/daily_prices/VIX9D_History.csv verified 200 with data back to 2011 (VIX3M_History.csv 215KB).
sources.free_tier: No registration, no API key, no license wall (verified by direct fetch and on the daily statistics page, which states data is furnished without responsibility for accuracy and lists no registration requirement). Delayed (15-min) data for informational/internal use; this project needs one snapshot per day. History CSVs are open downloads.
sources.roi_rationale: Portfolio-wide effect for a ~100-line fetcher: regime detection currently keys off a SINGLE spot-VIX rolling quantile (backend/backtest/regime_detector.py) and macro_regime.py sees only FRED VIXCLS. The VIX9D/VIX/VIX3M slope (backwardation vs contango) is the canonical vol-regime confirmation and leads spot-VIX quantile flips; equity-only put/call is a standard contrarian sentiment gauge the repo's per-ticker yfinance options tool cannot see (no market-wide aggregate). Regime state gates sizing and rotation for every position, so signal quality here compounds across the whole book. History CSVs allow immediate OOS validation against the frozen promotion gates without waiting for live accumulation. NOTE: persist to a NEW pyfinagent_data table -- historical_macro is operator-frozen.
sources.integration_point: backend/backtest/regime_detector.py::VIXRollingQuantileRegimeDetector (add term-structure slope confirm) + backend/services/macro_regime.py (add to the indicator dict beside VIXCLS/T10Y2Y/BAMLH0A0HYM2); new micro-fetcher in backend/tools/ persisting to a new pyfinagent_data table

sources.name: KRX short-selling balance + foreign-ownership data (data.krx.co.kr via pykrx / KRX Open API)
sources.what_it_provides: For the LIVE Korea market: daily short-selling balance per ticker (Korea discloses short BALANCES daily -- strictly richer than the US bi-monthly cadence), daily short-sale volume, top-50 shorted list, foreign-ownership shareholding and limit-exhaustion rates, and trading value/volume by investor type (foreign/institution/individual net buy). pykrx (github.com/sharebook-kr/pykrx, MIT) wraps these as get_shorting_balance_by_date, get_shorting_volume_by_ticker, get_exhaustion_rates_of_foreign_investment, get_market_trading_value_by_investor. KRX Data Marketplace OPEN API covers stocks/indices/short-selling statistics from 2010+.
sources.free_tier: KRX Open API: free account signup + free API auth-key issuance via MyPage (openapi.krx.co.kr; fees apply only to premium marketplace products, not the statistics API). pykrx path: free open-source scraping of data.krx.co.kr; some endpoints now require a free KRX member login (KRX_ID/KRX_PW env vars); library etiquette guidance is ~1 request/second. Data may differ slightly from official certified feeds per pykrx README.
sources.roi_rationale: The KR market is live in PAPER_MARKETS but trades on yfinance OHLCV alone -- zero flow, zero short, zero ownership data (verified: backend/backtest/markets.py maps KR:005930 -> 005930.KS and nothing else KR-specific exists). Foreign net-flow is the single most-studied return driver in Korean equities, and daily short-balance disclosure enables a crowding/exclusion signal the US side only gets bi-monthly. This is the largest untouched alpha surface per market the engine actually trades; effort is moderate (clone finra_short.py's fetch/normalize/upsert shape around pykrx calls). Watch the SecretStr trap when wiring KRX_ID/KRX_PW through settings.
sources.integration_point: New backend/alt_data/krx_short.py mirroring backend/alt_data/finra_short.py -> new BQ table (pyfinagent_data.alt_krx_short) -> consumed by backend/tools/screener.py exclusion for KR:* names (market-aware via backend/backtest/markets.py) and IC-tested in backend/alt_data/features.py

sources.name: iShares daily ETF product files (holdings CSV + shares outstanding) -- reactivates the etf_flows.py stub
sources.what_it_provides: Daily per-fund holdings CSV (verified live: the product-page .ajax?fileType=csv&fileName=IVV_holdings&dataType=fund endpoint returned HTTP 200 text/csv, 2.2MB constituent-level rows) plus on-page NAV and shares outstanding for every iShares ETF; SSGA publishes equivalent daily files for SPY/XL* sector funds. Day-over-day shares-outstanding delta x NAV = daily net creation/redemption flow -- exactly the formula already coded in backend/alt_data/etf_flows.py (flow_usd = (shares_out - shares_out_prev) * nav).
sources.free_tier: Public downloads from issuer product pages: no API key, no registration, no paywall (verified by direct fetch). Issuer terms permit personal/internal informational use; the project's own compliance record (docs/compliance/alt-data.md row 7.4) already approves this exact scrape at a 1 request / 2 seconds cap on public issuer pages.
sources.roi_rationale: This module is ~90% built and 100% dead: table DDL (alt_etf_flows), flow formula, rate limiter, upsert, CLI, and a 24-ticker starter universe (SPY/QQQ/sector XL* funds) all shipped in phase-7.4 -- fetch_issuer_page() returning {} is the ONLY missing piece (verified by reading the file). Sector-fund creation/redemption flows are a direct flow-data beat: they proxy institutional sector rotation, complementing the engine's sector caps and candidate selection. Effort is one parser; payoff is a daily flow factor across the exact ETFs that define the engine's sector map (backend/tools/sector_analysis.py uses the same 11 SPDRs).
sources.integration_point: backend/alt_data/etf_flows.py::fetch_issuer_page (implement against the verified iShares .ajax CSV + product-page NAV/shares-outstanding; SSGA files for SPY/XL*) -> existing pyfinagent_data.alt_etf_flows table -> IC harness in backend/alt_data/features.py -> sector-tilt context for backend/backtest/candidate_selector.py

sources.name: Wikimedia Pageviews REST API (attention ensemble alongside Google Trends)
sources.what_it_provides: Per-article daily (and hourly) pageview counts with bot filtering (agent=user) for any Wikipedia article, 2015-present -- verified live today keyless: /metrics/pageviews/per-article/en.wikipedia/all-access/user/Nvidia/daily/... returned daily view counts. Company-page attention is an investor-attention proxy with 10+ years of history, mappable ticker->article once and cached.
sources.free_tier: No API key required. Anonymous limit 100 requests/second per IP (500 req/s authenticated) per Wikimedia API rate-limit policy (mediawiki.org/wiki/Wikimedia_APIs/Rate_limits); a descriptive User-Agent with contact info is mandatory (generic UAs may be blocked). Pageview count data is freely licensed/public-domain aggregate data.
sources.roi_rationale: The engine's live attention signal (backend/tools/alt_data.py get_google_trends, consumed by the api/signals.py aggregator and the orchestrator pipeline on every ticker analysis) runs on ARCHIVED pytrends against an undocumented ~200 req/day per-IP Google quota with known sustained-429 degradations to UNAVAILABLE -- a single fragile leg. The repo's own gap audit (backend/data_audit/gaps.json, search_interest row) already prescribes exactly this: 'add Wikipedia pageviews as backup (free; zero auth)'. Effort is trivial (same 24h TTL cache pattern, ~150 lines); payoff is de-risking an already-consumed signal plus a second, independently-measured attention series for ensemble z-scoring, with clean history for IC validation.
sources.integration_point: backend/tools/alt_data.py (new get_wikipedia_pageviews beside get_google_trends, same module-level 24h TTL cache) -> backend/api/signals.py aggregator + backend/agents/orchestrator.py alt-data step; IC-testable via backend/alt_data/features.py

sources.name: SEC Fails-to-Deliver (FTD) data
sources.what_it_provides: Twice-monthly CNS fails-to-deliver files: per-ticker settlement-date fail quantity and price for every security with aggregate fails >= 10,000 shares (verified live: sec.gov/files/data/fails-deliver-data/cnsfails202506a.zip returned HTTP 200, 1.25MB zip of pipe-delimited CSV). Persistent/rising FTDs flag settlement stress, hard-to-borrow conditions, and squeeze-prone crowding -- the missing third leg beside daily short volume and bi-monthly short interest.
sources.free_tier: US-government public-domain data; no key, no registration, no rate tier -- standard SEC fair-access etiquette applies (declared User-Agent with contact info, which the repo already implements for EDGAR in backend/alt_data/f13.py; <=10 req/s ceiling is moot for 2 files/month).
sources.roi_rationale: Cheapest candidate on this list (download zip -> parse pipe-CSV -> dict, mirroring the existing short_interest.py loader shape) and it feeds an already-live consumer: the screener exclusion filter. Combined with restored FINRA short interest (#1) and the orphaned daily short-volume table, it completes a squeeze/liquidity-stress guard for the long book -- entries into names with distorted, crowded-short microstructure are exactly the fills a paper engine mis-prices vs reality, so excluding them protects the OOS-robustness of measured P&L. Modest standalone alpha, near-zero effort, zero regression risk (pure additive exclusion input).
sources.integration_point: backend/services/short_interest.py-style loader (new backend/services/ftd_data.py or a second lookup in the same module) -> merged into the short_interest_lookup exclusion dict consumed by backend/tools/screener.py; joinable with pyfinagent_data.alt_finra_short_volume in backend/alt_data/features.py for IC testing

vetted.title: SEC EDGAR full-text search API (efts.sec.gov) — re-activation of stubbed 13D/13G leg
vetted.notes: Aggregator + Legs 1-2 already LIVE: compute_ma_preannounce_signals is wired in autonomous_loop.py:452-455 and applied in screener.py:407-409. Leg 3 is a genuine stub returning [] (ma_preannounce_screen.py:55-74, TODO phase-28.16-followup-13d-edgar). SEC UA + rate infra to copy exists (sec_insider.py:20 SEC_HEADERS; alt_data/f13.py 8 req/s). Free/local/compatible — SEC public-domain, no historical_macro touch. BUT the ROI claim 'highest P&L-per-effort' is overstated: the whole ma_preannounce feature is DEFAULT OFF (settings.py:504 ma_preannounce_enabled=False) and unproven, and it's only a +5/+10% score-boost overlay on rare M&A-convergence events. Effort is genuinely tiny (~50 lines, all infra present); P&L is speculative and gated behind an off-by-default flag. The proposal's own code comment attributes the 403 to needing an authenticated client, which the proposal reconciles as a missing-UA artifact — plausible given f13.py's working EDGAR UA, but not independently confirmed here.

vetted.title: Finnhub free-tier endpoints not yet consumed (insider transactions/sentiment, recommendation trends, per-symbol news, earnings surprises, as-reported financials)
vetted.notes: Verified: exactly 2 Finnhub endpoints consumed — /news?category=general (news/sources/finnhub.py) and /calendar/earnings (econ_calendar/sources/finnhub_earnings.py). Proposed endpoints (recommendation, insider-sentiment, company-news, metric, earnings) are all unused. Infra all present: settings.finnhub_api_key (settings.py:125), get_rate_limiter('finnhub'), fail-open adapter pattern. analyst_revisions.py confirmed rides SOLELY on fragile yfinance upgrades_downgrades — an official keyed second source is a real de-risking of a live Sharpe~1.6 overlay; earnings-surprise feeds PEAD SUE at $0 LLM. Near-zero effort. CAVEAT the operator should weigh: Finnhub's free tier is broadly US-equities-only for company data — the proposal's '/stock/recommendation international coverage' claim is optimistic; free international breadth is doubtful, so the KR/EU sleeves likely won't benefit. Compatible with policy (free key already provisioned, local, no macro table).

vetted.title: SEC XBRL data APIs — companyfacts / companyconcept / frames (data.sec.gov)
vetted.notes: Highest-quality of the six. Verified XBRL companyfacts/frames entirely UNUSED (only data.sec.gov/submissions + Archives are touched in sec_insider.py; quant_model.py has NO companyfacts leg despite an orchestrator.py:1635 comment implying one). pead_signal.py currently computes an LLM (Haiku) sentiment-surprise (~$0.05/cycle), NOT an XBRL earnings SUE — so a deterministic $0-LLM revenue-surprise/SUE factor is genuinely additive and STRENGTHENS an already-working PEAD edge rather than adding speculative alpha. Explicitly prescribed by the project's own audit: data_audit/gaps.json line 61-62 ('build a 10-Q revenue-surprise signal from existing sec_edgar data'). CIK resolution reuses sec_insider.py::_resolve_cik. Free/compatible: SEC public-domain, 10 req/s, nightly bulk ZIP, filed-date stamps = backtest-safe (no yfinance revision leakage), new feature tables (no historical_macro touch). Effort moderate (parse companyfacts JSON, compute SUE). Best fit to the OOS-robustness north star.

vetted.title: Korea DART OpenAPI (Financial Supervisory Service)
vetted.notes: Verified genuinely absent: zero opendart/crtfc references; the KR sleeve trades on yfinance OHLCV with no KR fundamentals/filings/insider. New backend/alt_data/dart.py + new alt_* tables is policy-compatible (free crtfc_key ~20k req/day, local, historical_macro untouched). BUT two ROI deductions: (1) the headline consumer it cites — data_integrity.py phase-60.3 market-cap verification — ALREADY works via yfinance info.currency + suffix-implied-currency + FX conversion (data_integrity.py:48-95, 120-133); DART would be a supplemental cross-check, not filling a hole in that gate; the away-week $1.6-quadrillion KRW error is already caught by the existing implausible_market_cap flag. (2) The real new value (KR insider/fundamentals alpha) is unproven, the KR book is smaller, and Korean-language key/portal friction raises effort. Moderate effort, speculative payoff on a smaller sleeve.

vetted.title: ECB Data Portal API (SDMX 2.1 REST)
vetted.notes: Verified absent from macro: macro_regime.py::_REGIME_SERIES is US-only (T10Y2Y, VIXCLS, BAMLH0A0HYM2); econ_calendar/sources/ holds only fred_releases/fed_scrape/finnhub_earnings; fx_rates.py has yfinance->FRED (_CCY_FRED) with no ECB third fallback. EU DAX-40 sleeve is live and gated by US regime, so the coverage gap is real. Registry + fallback patterns exist. Free (keyless SDMX 2.1) and policy-compatible (file-cache or new table, historical_macro untouched). ROI held to 4: (a) this entry is MIS-FILED under 'fundamentals-filings' — it's a macro-rates source and is DUPLICATED, with more detail (CISS), as source #3 of the macro-rates-positioning beat, so vet/build it once there; (b) it's an unproven regime-multiplier overlay on the smaller EU sleeve; moderate effort for a soft risk-gating benefit.

vetted.title: Wikimedia Pageviews API (AQS REST)
vetted.notes: Trivial effort (keyless, one URL template, reuse 24h cache) and prescribed by the repo's own audit (data_audit/gaps.json:70-71 and desired.json:93 'add Wikipedia pageviews as backup'). But low expected P&L: attention/search-interest is a low-conviction signal, and the framing is muddled — the entry claims google_trends 'remains a scaffold with live fetch deferred', but the LIVE attention path is tools/alt_data.py::get_google_trends (pytrends 4.9.2 with 24h TTL, actually consumed by api/signals.py:93,164 and orchestrator.py:1186); the dead scaffold is the separate alt_data/google_trends.py. So this proposal builds an OFFLINE IC-measurement backup (persist to alt_* + features.py) for a signal class whose primary already runs. Net: near-zero effort, near-zero P&L — justified only as robustness/measurement scaffolding, not earnings. Not a build-now item.

vetted.title: FRED — missing regime/liquidity series on the existing key (INDPRO, NFCI, WALCL, WTREGEN, RRPONTSYD, DTWEXBGS, T10Y3M)
vetted.notes: FRED plumbing is fully live (fred_data.py::SERIES fetches 9 series; consumed by macro_regime.py + screener.py:318), but the SPECIFIC proposed series are not fetched. INDPRO claim CONFIRMED as a real latent defect: grep shows INDPRO appears ONLY at macro_regime.py:43 (_REGIME_SERIES) and is absent from fred_data.py::SERIES; _build_prompt (line 357) skips series not present in `indicators` (the `continue` guard), so the regime LLM demonstrably runs on 6 of its 7 intended series. NFCI/WALCL/WTREGEN/RRPONTSYD/DTWEXBGS/T10Y3M: zero references anywhere in backend. Policy-compatible: FRED_API_KEY already configured, 120 req/min, no new dep/table; proposal correctly routes to the live-path dicts (fred_data.SERIES + _REGIME_SERIES + prompt thresholds, 24h file cache, no BQ) and explicitly NOT to data_ingestion.py:22 FRED_SERIES, which I confirmed is the sole writer of the FROZEN historical_macro (lines 272-291). Free tier plausible (already in use). Highest ROI of the six: repairs a confirmed bug in the module that scales every candidate daily + adds net-liquidity/NFCI (most-cited risk-on/off drivers) at ~10 lines, zero auth, zero new table. Caveat: incremental regime-multiplier P&L is a coarse global scalar and the new series' prompt thresholds need calibration (not truly zero-effort), so I discount from 9 to 8. The INDPRO fix alone justifies doing this regardless of the new-alpha series.

vetted.title: ECB Data Portal API (SDMX 2.1 REST) — euro-area regime series + EUR reference rates
vetted.notes: Zero euro-area macro in repo (grep clean for data-api.ecb / CISS / estr — only a doc-comment 'post-ECB QE' in gauntlet/regimes.py:125). The live-capital gap is REAL and CONFIRMED: EU sleeve trades ^GDAXI (markets.py:48-53), yet screener.py:318-321 feeds one global US-FRED regime (apply_regime_to_score) to EVERY candidate — EU names are risk-gated by the wrong central bank. Policy-compatible: keyless SDMX REST, new backend/tools/ecb_data.py (file cache, no BQ) or a NEW macro_eu_series table (historical_macro untouched); fx_rates.py 2-source chain (_CCY_YF→_CCY_FRED, confirmed lines 38/46) can take an ECB reference-rate 3rd fallback; econ_calendar registry.register() + fred_releases.py sibling confirmed for ECB meeting dates. Free tier plausible; record honestly flags 503s → retry/backoff. Highest-value NEW source: closes the largest coverage hole relative to live capital, and CISS is a single clean daily number that fits the threshold-prompt design. IMPORTANT CAVEAT the proposal understates: adding 'an EU block in _build_prompt' only produces ONE blended global regime — it does NOT give EU candidates an EU-specific multiplier. True per-market risk-gating needs compute_macro_regime + apply_regime_to_score to become market-aware (architectural change, more than 'one fetcher'). Even a blended EU+US regime beats US-only for the EU book, so 7, but the effort is larger than advertised.

vetted.title: Bank of Korea ECOS Open API
vetted.notes: Zero KR macro in repo (grep clean for ecos.bok / bank of korea / bok word-boundary). Same structural hole as ECB, CONFIRMED for a live sleeve: KR trades ^KS11 (markets.py:55-59) but gets the US-FRED regime multiplier via screener.py:318-321. Policy-compatible: free registered key, JSON/XML, new backend/tools/bok_ecos.py (file cache), optional NEW macro_kr_series table (historical_macro frozen), BOK MPC dates via the confirmed register() pattern. Free-tier claim is the WEAKEST of the six by the record's own admission: numeric daily quota could not be confirmed from a fetchable English page (Korean-language JS portal only) — honest but unverified; verify at key-issuance. Ranked below ECB because (a) smaller KR book = lower P&L stakes, (b) higher friction (Korean docs, key application, quota uncertainty), (c) same market-aware-regime wiring problem as ECB. Genuine live-capital coverage gap, so mid ROI.

vetted.title: CFTC Commitments of Traders — Public Reporting Environment (Socrata SODA API)
vetted.notes: Positioning beat genuinely absent: grep finds only two doc-comment mentions of 'CFTC' as a historical authority in backend/backtest/gauntlet/regimes.py (2010 flash-crash cite), zero data ingestion, 'cot' word-boundary empty. Clone target CONFIRMED (congress.py::ensure_table/upsert_trades line 234/249; finra_short.py same shape) and features.py::compute_ic (Spearman IC over {5,20,63}-day, lines 248-286) exists for evaluation. Policy-compatible: no token required (optional free Socrata app token), NEW alt_cftc_cot table (historical_macro frozen), weekly one-fetch cadence. Free tier plausible. Discounted to 4 because: (1) the proposed consumer is a poor fit — _apply_gpr_tilt (macro_regime.py:290) ONLY injects a fixed sector-ETF list into sector_hints.overweight when above_threshold; index/Treasury/VIX positioning extremes are a market-wide crowding signal, NOT a sector overweight, so 'reuse _apply_gpr_tilt' is mechanically true but semantically wrong — a real consumer needs design work; (2) COT is as-of-Tuesday released Friday (3-day lag), weekly, index-level, and heavily studied/arbitraged, so standalone equity-index alpha is modest. Novel dimension but speculative P&L per unit of real integration effort.

vetted.title: US Treasury FiscalData API (Daily Treasury Statement + Treasury auction results)
vetted.notes: Zero references in repo (grep clean for fiscaldata / TGA / daily treasury / bid-to-cover). Policy-compatible and the cheapest auth story of the six: fully open REST, no key; daily TGA into the macro_regime net-liquidity computation (file cache, no BQ, historical_macro frozen); auction dates as a new econ_calendar source via the confirmed register()/CalendarSource Protocol (registry.py:19-30, fred_releases.py:103) with event_type='treasury_auction'. Free tier highly plausible (US-gov public domain). Discounted to 4 because it is a refinement-of-a-refinement, not a coverage hole: (1) daily TGA only adds value once WALCL−WTREGEN−RRPONTSYD net-liquidity is built — i.e. CONTINGENT on FRED source #1 landing first, and that net-liquidity construct does not exist yet; (2) it deepens the US regime, which is already the best-covered market, rather than the uncovered live EU/KR sleeves; (3) daily-vs-weekly TGA resolution is a marginal edge. Auction-event calendar is the one genuinely new capability. Novel but low incremental P&L.

vetted.title: EIA Open Data API v2 (Weekly Petroleum Status — crude/product inventories)
vetted.notes: EIA data itself is absent (grep clean for eia.gov / api.eia / petroleum), but the CONSUMER path already exists and works: macro_regime.py::_fetch_crude_momentum (phase-28.6, CL=F price-momentum z-score) already tilts XLE via _apply_gpr_tilt, and here the _apply_gpr_tilt reuse IS a genuine fit (it injects XLE, exactly what an inventory-surprise trigger wants). Policy-compatible: free key, new _fetch_eia_inventories() helper beside _fetch_crude_momentum, file cache, one settings flag default-OFF — the house pattern is CONFIRMED (gpr_signal_enabled/crude_momentum_enabled both Field(False) in settings.py:412/417). Free tier plausible (one weekly fetch). Marked LOW-VALUE (the proposal's own 'lowest rank') because marginal P&L is the smallest of the six: (1) it upgrades one narrow single-sector tilt (XLE ~3% of a diversified book), not a coverage hole; (2) crucially, the sibling price-momentum trigger it augments is default-OFF (crude_momentum_enabled=False), so this adds a second orthogonal leg to a feature that is not even enabled in production — value is near-zero until the base feature is validated/turned on; (3) weekly inventory-surprise alpha is well-arbitraged. Effort is genuinely tiny, but so is the payoff.

vetted.title: FINRA Consolidated Short Interest via Query API (replaces dead legacy CSV)
vetted.notes: REPAIR, not new ingestion. backend/services/short_interest.py IS live-wired to the screener exclusion filter (autonomous_loop.py:340-341 -> screener.py:185-190, threshold 0.10, Boehmer-Jones-Zhang basis) and the CDN URL it hits is line 36 (_FINRA_CSV_BASE cdn.finra.org/.../shrt{date}.csv); on failure it falls back to yfinance for missing[:50] (lines 206-210), matching the 'degraded to 50-ticker fallback' claim. Two honest caveats: (1) the consumer is gated by short_interest_filter_enabled DEFAULT FALSE (settings.py:402), so 'provably running blind/live today' is contingent on the operator having flipped the flag; (2) NOT a pure URL swap -- the Query API JSON exposes currentShortPositionQuantity/averageDailyVolumeQuantity/daysToCoverQuantity but NO percent-of-float field, so the parser (_parse_finra_csv keys on a 'float'/'percent' column) needs a real float-derivation or a threshold-semantics change. Policy: free public dataset (no credential), local, no new table (optionally backfills the orphaned alt_finra_short_volume). Highest certainty-of-impact in this beat: existing consumer, fail-open, low regression risk. roi 7 tempered by the default-OFF contingency and non-trivial parser work.

vetted.title: CBOE delayed index quotes + daily market statistics (VIX term structure and put/call ratios)
vetted.notes: Genuinely NOT ingested. macro_regime.py's live regime uses only spot VIXCLS from FRED (_REGIME_SERIES line 42); backend/backtest/regime_detector.py is a single ^VIX rolling-quantile classifier AND is a backtest-side spot-check component gated regime_detection_enabled DEFAULT OFF; options_flow.py computes put/call PER-TICKER from yfinance, never a market-wide aggregate. VIX9D/VIX/VIX3M term-structure slope + market-wide equity put/call are absent. Policy-clean: keyless delayed JSON + open history CSVs (free), local, proposal correctly persists to a NEW table (historical_macro frozen). Effort ~100-line fetcher + wiring into TWO consumers (regime_detector + macro_regime) + a table. Portfolio-wide regime signal that compounds across sizing/rotation, and history CSVs enable OOS validation -- but it is speculative alpha (not a repair), regime-signal -> P&L conversion is notoriously uncertain, and the live regime already has spot VIX, so this is incremental confirmation. roi 6.

vetted.title: KRX short-selling balance + foreign-ownership data (data.krx.co.kr via pykrx / KRX Open API)
vetted.notes: Genuinely absent: zero KRX/pykrx references anywhere; the live KR sleeve trades on yfinance OHLCV alone (markets.py maps KR:005930 -> 005930.KS and nothing else). Largest untouched alpha surface on a market the engine ACTUALLY trades -- foreign net-flow is the single most-studied KR return driver and daily short-BALANCE disclosure is strictly richer than the US bi-monthly cadence. But P&L-per-effort is dragged down by real execution risk: pykrx is open-source scraping of data.krx.co.kr (fragile; README warns data may differ from certified feeds), some endpoints now require a free KRX login (KRX_ID/KRX_PW -> the documented SecretStr truthiness trap that already killed 4 overlays), Korean-language quota docs, a smaller KR book, plus new-table + market-aware screener wiring (screener exclusion is currently US-only). Free tier plausible (free signup + auth-key; fees only on premium marketplace products). Moderate-to-high effort/fragility on a smaller sleeve -> roi 5.

vetted.title: iShares daily ETF product files (holdings CSV + shares outstanding) -- reactivates the etf_flows.py stub
vetted.notes: Scaffold is real but the ROI claim is OVERSTATED. backend/alt_data/etf_flows.py ships the table DDL (alt_etf_flows), derive_flow formula, rate limiter, upsert, CLI, and a 24-ticker starter universe; fetch_issuer_page returns {} (line 69-78) -- confirmed. Compliance row 7.4 in docs/compliance/alt-data.md approves the issuer-page scrape (1 req/s per domain). HOWEVER the proposal's 'fetch_issuer_page is the ONLY missing piece' is false for P&L purposes: alt_etf_flows has ZERO consumers (grep: only a comment in alt_data/http.py; no IC-harness read, no candidate_selector tilt, ingest_tickers has no external caller). So implementing the parser lands rows in a table nobody reads -- you must ALSO build the consumer wiring the proposal hand-waves. Dead since phase-7.4. Speculative flow alpha over a fragile, undocumented .ajax endpoint (subject to change/blocking). Policy-compatible (free public downloads, existing table). Low P&L-per-effort once the true (parser + consumer) scope is counted -> roi 4.

vetted.title: Wikimedia Pageviews REST API (attention ensemble alongside Google Trends)
vetted.notes: The DATA (Wikipedia pageviews) is not ingested -- the repo's Wikipedia refs are all S&P 500 constituent scraping (screener.py) -- but the SIGNAL CLASS (retail attention) is already LIVE via get_google_trends (tools/alt_data.py), consumed on every ticker by orchestrator.py:1186 and api/signals.py:93/164. This is a redundant SECOND source for an already-covered class. gaps.json literally prescribes it (search_interest row: 'add Wikipedia pageviews as backup') but rates it severity LOW (score 60). Policy-clean: keyless, free, in-module 24h TTL cache -> no new BQ table strictly needed. Effort trivial (~150 lines, copy the existing cache pattern), but marginal net P&L is near-zero: it only de-risks a signal that ALREADY degrades gracefully (get_google_trends returns UNAVAILABLE not ERROR on 429), and attention is weak standalone alpha. Robustness/ensemble value only -> roi 3.

vetted.title: SEC Fails-to-Deliver (FTD) data
vetted.notes: Genuinely absent -- zero FTD/cnsfails/fails-to-deliver references. Completes the short-microstructure triad beside the live short_interest.py and the orphaned daily alt_finra_short_volume table. Cheapest ingest on the list (twice-monthly zip -> pipe-CSV -> dict, mirroring short_interest.py's loader) and the SEC User-Agent discipline already exists in alt_data/f13.py. Policy-clean: US-gov public domain, no key, <=10 req/s moot for 2 files/month. Two caveats keep it modest: (1) it feeds the SAME screener exclusion consumer that is gated default-OFF (short_interest_filter_enabled); (2) FTD is a raw share COUNT, not a percent-of-float, so 'merge into the short_interest_lookup dict' is not a clean drop-in -- it needs its own thresholding/normalization logic, not just a second lookup. Modest orthogonal squeeze/liquidity-stress alpha, near-zero effort, additive/low-regression -> roi 5.
